title: Modular browser application

canonicalExplanation.summary: A browser app integrates modules, semantic UI, state, async boundaries, security, tests, build, and deployment paths into one traceable product flow.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A small tracker handles loading, empty, success, validation, server failure, retry, keyboard, and narrow-screen states. Your goal is to deliver and explain a keyboard-usable modular browser application with tested loading, empty, success, error and stale-request states. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Browser state and security; Dependency boundaries; DOM and asynchronous integration tests; DOM injection prevention; css project system. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A browser app integrates modules, semantic UI, state, async boundaries, security, tests, build, and deployment paths into one traceable product flow. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: A working local happy path can hide offline, empty, error, stale, accessibility, privacy, and subpath failures. This draft therefore rejects: Completing the primary success screen means a browser project is production-ready.

includes: Integrate semantic HTML, modules, state, DOM, forms, fetch, errors, security and tests.
Deliver and explain a keyboard-usable modular browser application with tested loading, empty, success, error and stale-request states.
excludes: A working local happy path can hide offline, empty, error, stale, accessibility, privacy, and subpath failures.

outcomes: Deliver and explain a keyboard-usable modular browser application with tested loading, empty, success, error and stale-request states.
